name: CODEXIA
subtitle: The Seven Spires
description: Trollmage's domain. Seven black-spire towers, one per sin-codex, arranged in a perfect ring. They hum in minor-key harmony. The central void between them is where the Sabbat Council meets.
ruler: Trollmage — Keeper of the Seven Sin Codices
gate: The Seven Gate (recite the codices backward)

life.phenomenon: It sometimes rains ink. The ink writes sentences on walls as it falls. The sentences are always TRUE and always WARNINGS. Ink-rain is how the universe whispers to Codexia.
life.inhabitants: Sin-Keepers (one per spire, seven total). Mages, necromancers, and the Sabbat Council. One permanently confused tourist nobody remembers inviting.

fauna.name: Sin Raven
fauna.note: Each raven carries one feather of one sin. Collect all seven feathers for a wish. Currently 6 are in circulation; the Envy feather has not been seen in 200 years.

fauna.name: Codex Bat
fauna.note: Hangs from book pages to memorize them. A flock of 40 can recite an entire tome in harmony.

fauna.name: Shadow Snake
fauna.note: Only exists in peripheral vision. Look directly and it's gone. Many Codexians have one as a companion, though none can prove it.

flora.name: Patchouli Orchard
flora.note: The trees smell of magic. Walk through barefoot and you remember a spell you've never cast.

flora.name: Inkvine
flora.note: Writes whatever it touches. Do not let them climb your walls unless you want your walls to have opinions.

life.architecture: Black marble spires with interior spiral libraries descending kilometers into the ground. The Dark Patois Dancehall has a neon sign visible from space. Every window faces inward toward the Sabbat Chamber.
life.culture: Greet others by confessing a MINOR sin. 'I ate my roommate's bread this morning' is polite. 'I have no sin' is grounds for immediate ejection — nobody is sinless, so you are lying, which is itself a sin.
life.signature_item: Sin-Ring — bind one sin temporarily. Wear the Greed ring and you cannot be stolen from. Wear the Envy ring and you do not want what others have (rare — bought only once per decade).

sensory.sound: Bell-hum of all 7 spires resonating in minor key. Occasional pages turning in distant libraries. Ink drops landing.
sensory.smell: Old books, patchouli, faint sulfur, occasionally a whiff of Dark Patois dancehall smoke.
sensory.feel: Paper-dry air. Fingers always slightly ink-stained even if you haven't written anything.

backdrop: <defs><radialGradient id='darkAura' cx='50%' cy='50%' r='50%'><stop offset='0%' stop-color='#4a1080' stop-opacity='0.25'/><stop offset='100%' stop-color='#0a0218' stop-opacity='0'/></radialGradient></defs><rect width='1600' height='1000' fill='url(#darkAura)'/><circle cx='800' cy='500' r='360' fill='none' stroke='#9933ff' stroke-width='1.5' opacity='0.25' stroke-dasharray='3 8'/><circle cx='800' cy='500' r='280' fill='none' stroke='#9933ff' stroke-width='1' opacity='0.18'/>

features.svg: <g><circle cx='800' cy='500' r='70' fill='#0a0030' stroke='#9933ff' stroke-width='2' opacity='0.7'/><circle cx='800' cy='500' r='70' fill='none' stroke='#c77aff' stroke-width='1' opacity='0.4' stroke-dasharray='2 6'><animateTransform attributeName='transform' type='rotate' from='0 800 500' to='360 800 500' dur='60s' repeatCount='indefinite'/></circle><text x='800' y='496' text-anchor='middle' fill='#c77aff' font-size='10' letter-spacing='3' opacity='0.8'>THE SABBAT</text><text x='800' y='512' text-anchor='middle' fill='#c77aff' font-size='9' letter-spacing='3' opacity='0.65'>COUNCIL CHAMBER</text></g>

cities.name: Spire of Pride
cities.note: Tallest of the seven. Warning at base: 'Read slowly.'
cities.lore: Pride hums the highest note. Those who approach without humility hear ringing in their ears for 3 days. Those with true humility hear nothing — the spire respects them.

cities.name: Spire of Greed
cities.note: The only spire with a lock. Key missing since 1971.
cities.lore: Around its base, fallen coins accumulate. No one picks them up — the coins refuse to be picked up. They belong to the spire now.

cities.name: Spire of Wrath
cities.note: Cracked down the middle. The crack does not widen or close.
cities.lore: The crack was caused by its own hum. Wrath is the only spire that cannot contain itself.

cities.name: Spire of Envy
cities.note: Mirror-polished black. Constantly reflects the other six.
cities.lore: Walk around Envy and every other spire appears slightly taller than it is. That is Envy doing its work.

cities.name: Spire of Lust
cities.note: Wrapped in vines. The vines bloom at dusk.
cities.lore: Flowers open only after sunset. No one has planted them. They do not need soil — they root in the marble.

cities.name: Spire of Gluttony
cities.note: Wider at the base than any other. Still growing wider.
cities.lore: Every 100 years, Gluttony's base expands by 3 meters. Architects have not yet decided if this is a problem.

cities.name: Spire of Sloth
cities.note: Leans at 4 degrees. Has for 600 years. Will not fall.
cities.lore: The laziest of the spires. Sloth does not bother to hum — but you still hear it. It's the sound of the other six, resonating through Sloth's stone.

cities.name: Dark Patois Dancehall
cities.note: The only place in Codexia where all seven sins are performed simultaneously, legally.
cities.lore: By ancient treaty, the dancehall operates outside the sin-tax. Every night. Open forever. Trollmage himself DJs on Thursdays.

sacred_sites.name: The Sabbat Chamber
sacred_sites.note: The void between the seven spires. Where Trollmage and six others convene.
sacred_sites.lore: The seventh chair is always empty. No one has ever sat in it. It belongs to whichever sin you came carrying.

sacred_sites.name: The Seventh Step
sacred_sites.note: The final step before entering the Sabbat Chamber.
sacred_sites.lore: You must speak your truest sin aloud to cross it. If you lie, you are returned to Scalelist mid-step, confused.
